title: eriogonum glandulosum
intro: Eriogonum glandulosum, also known as Mormon Tea or Redstem Filaree, is a shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eriogonum glandulosum include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying the plant material to make a supplement or tincture.
